title: Bridge Essay: Enlarged Interior Built Environment Scale Modulates High-Frequency EEG Oscillati

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Breath/Soul** (Phase 2) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Breath/Soul to mechanistic neuroscience.

**Enlarged Interior Built Environment Scale Modulates High-Frequency EEG Oscillations.** — eNeuro. PMID: 36028331. DOI: 10.1523/ENEURO.0104-22.2022. There is currently no robust method to evaluate how built environment design affects our emotion. Understanding emotion is significant, as it influences cognitive processes, behavior, and wellbeing, and is linked to the functioning of physiological systems. As mental health problems are becoming more prevalent, and exposure to indoor environments is increasing, it is important we develop rigorous methods to understand whether design elements in our environment affect emotion.
